title: Intense Midnight Turkish Bottarga Flatbread
description: A malty, velvety Turkish pide with braised bottarga that satisfies midnight cravings while staying FODMAP-friendly.
instructions: In a bowl combine flour, yeast, salt and malt syrup until mixed.
Add warm water and olive oil, stirring until dough forms.
Knead on a floured surface until smooth and elastic, about eight minutes.
Place dough in a greased bowl, cover and let rise until doubled, about one hour.
Preheat oven to 220C and line a baking sheet with parchment.
Punch down dough, roll into a 30cm oval and place on the sheet.
Melt ghee in a skillet over medium heat and add bottarga, smoked paprika, cumin and lemon zest, cooking until fragrant, about two minutes.
Spread the bottarga mixture over the flatbread and drizzle with a little extra olive oil.
Bake for ten to twelve minutes until golden and crisp.
Garnish with chopped parsley, slice and serve hot.
